Ntc And Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Apr-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LYDUMILA PASTERNAK, and, ALBERT STOLL JR. Motion for New Trial. Appearances: Gregory D. Brown, Esq. Deputy Attorney General, Dept. of Justice, (415) 703-5461, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Christian Einfeldt, Esq. of Stoll Law Office (415) 576-1500 appearing for defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for new trial is denied as to all of the grounds raised. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Angie Diner CSR# 9581

Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of The 02/26/10 Order Determinating Lien Amt. Pursuant To Welfare & Institutions Code 14124.76
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LYDUMILA PASTERNAK, and, ALBERT STOLL JR. Motion for Reconsideration of the 02/26/10 Order Determining Lien Amount Pursuant to Welfare & Institutions Code 14124.76. Appearances: Christian Einfeldt, Esq. of Stoll Law Office (415) 576-1500 appearing for appearing for defendants and Moving party; Gregory D. Brown, Deputy Attorney General, State of California, (415) 703-5461 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Lopez vs. Daimler Chrysler Corporation 179 Cal.App. 4th 1373 is not "new" law and could have been cited at the time the reply brief was prepared and filed at the hearing. Furthermore, Lopez does not alter the burden of proof. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
